report:
Nutriva Corp – Comprehensive Investment Memo
Date: June 15, 2026

I. Company Overview and Business Model

– Nutriva Corp is a U.S.-based provider of biotech-enhanced nutritional supplements targeting metabolic health and cognitive function, founded in 2014, headquartered in Austin, Texas.
– Private company with $620 million in revenue as of FY 2025.
– Nutriva’s core products include:
  • Proprietary line of daily dietary supplements enhanced with lab-validated adaptive peptides and nootropics.
  • Subscription-based personalized nutrition service using AI-driven metabolic profiling to tailor supplement bundles.
  • B2B channel supplying branded formulations to wellness clinics and gyms.
– Nutriva has experienced rapid revenue growth driven by consumer health trends and recurring subscription revenue.

II. Market Landscape and Competitive Environment

– Nutriva operates within the global nutraceutical market, valued at $250 billion in 2025 growing ~8% CAGR.
– Key competitors:
  • VitalCore Labs: older brand, general wellness supplements, ~$450M revenue, less personalized.
  • MindWell Nutrition: focuses on cognitive supplements, ~$320M revenue, limited personalization.
  • BioAdapt Health: smaller player specializing in peptide-based supplements, $120M revenue.
– Nutriva differentiates through proprietary biotech peptides and AI customization.
– Market drivers include rising consumer focus on personalized health, cognitive longevity, and metabolic wellness.

III. Customers and Distribution

– Customer base:
  • 1.3 million active subscribers as of Q1 2026, 78% subscriber retention rate.
  • Consumer demographic mainly 30–55 years old, health-conscious urban professionals.
  • B2B partnerships include 250+ health centers and fitness franchises.
– Distribution channels:
  • Direct-to-consumer (DTC) online subscriptions (~81% of revenue).
  • Retail partnerships (health food stores, pharmacies) (~12%).
  • B2B sales (~7%).
– Customer acquisition cost (CAC) reported at $45, with average customer lifetime value (LTV) of $675.

IV. Financial Performance (FY 2023–2025)

(in millions USD)

| Fiscal Year | Revenue | Gross Margin | EBITDA Margin | Net Income |
|-------------|---------|--------------|---------------|------------|
| 2023        | 410     | 64%          | 18%           | 44         |
| 2024        | 530     | 65%          | 22%           | 71         |
| 2025        | 620     | 66%          | 24%           | 89         |

– Revenue grew at ~26% CAGR (2023–2025).
– Gross margin steadily improved driven by optimized peptide synthesis and direct sales.
– EBITDA margin expanded from 18% to 24%; net income margin improved to ~14%.
– Operating expenses remain stable relative to revenue, reflecting scalable AI platform.
– Strong free cash flow generation: $110M FCF in 2025 (18% FCF margin).

Balance Sheet Highlights (end FY 2025):
– Cash and equivalents: $150M
– Debt: $95M (lower-cost term loans; debt/EBITDA ~1.1x)
– Working capital: $130M
– Intangible assets include proprietary peptide formulations.

V. Technology and Product Innovation

– Proprietary library of over 50 patented adaptive peptides targeting metabolic and neuro pathways.
– AI-driven metabolic profiling platform integrating genomics, microbiome, and lifestyle data to personalize formulations.
– Continuous R&D pipeline targeting expansion into immune health peptides.
– Scalable production with key partnerships at GMP-certified manufacturing sites.

VI. Growth Opportunities

– Geographic expansion into Europe and Asia-Pacific planned for 2027–28, targeting growing wellness markets.
– Expanded B2B partnerships with healthcare providers and corporate wellness programs.
– Potential M&A to acquire complementary microbiome supplement technologies.
– Broader platform-based subscription upsells including telehealth nutritional guidance.
– Operational efficiencies expected from manufacturing scale and AI cost reductions.

VII. Risks and Mitigation

– Regulatory landscape for novel peptide supplements evolving; active compliance program in place.
– Competitive pressure from emerging biotech nutrition startups.
– Dependency on continuous innovation to maintain differentiation.
– Customer retention can be challenged by pricing and competitor offers.

VIII. Summary

Nutriva Corp is a fast-growing biotech nutrition company with strong proprietary technology, solid financials centered on recurring revenue, and a defensible niche in personalized metabolic and cognitive health supplements. The company’s AI-driven personalization and patented peptides provide a sustainable competitive advantage in a large and growing market. Expansion initiatives and product pipeline support continued robust growth and margin expansion.
reasoning:
Okay, looking at Nutriva Corp, the company operates in the fast-growing nutraceutical space with a clear focus on biotech-enhanced supplements targeting metabolic and cognitive health. That sector is expanding robustly due to increasing consumer health consciousness, so the market tailwinds are strong here.

Let me analyze the financials: Nutriva's revenue grew from $410M in 2023 to $620M in 2025, a 26% CAGR, which is quite healthy for this sector. The gross margin improvement to 66% indicates good scalability and pricing power, likely due to proprietary peptides and direct sales channels. EBITDA and net income margins expanding to mid-20%s and ~14% respectively show strong operational leverage. The company also generates solid free cash flow at 18% of revenue, illustrating financial strength and capital efficiency.

On competition, Nutriva differentiates itself with a patented peptide library and an AI-driven metabolic profiling platform. Competitors are notable but less personalized and with smaller scale or lower tech focus, which supports Nutriva's defensibility. The recurring subscription model with strong retention (78%) and attractive LTV/CAC ratio further support customer quality and revenue visibility.

Growth opportunities appear substantial: international expansion into Europe and Asia-Pacific markets, expanding B2B partnerships, new product pipeline targets like immune health, and possible M&A opportunities. The AI platform and peptide tech create barriers to entry and ongoing innovation capacity.

Risks include evolving regulations on peptide supplements and maintaining continuous innovation to stay ahead. Customer retention and pricing are always risks in consumer products but the data indicates strong stickiness so far.

Overall, Nutriva looks like a profitable, well-positioned growth company with technological moats and large market opportunity.

This company seems to check many boxes for a strong private equity investment: scalable technology, recurring high-margin revenue, strong growth and margin trends, defensible IP, experienced management implied, and clear growth runway.

verdict.evidence_pro:
1. Rapid revenue growth at 26% CAGR with expanding gross margins (up to 66%) and improving EBITDA (24%) and net income margins (~14%) illustrating strong profitability and operational leverage.
2. Proprietary patented biotech peptide library combined with AI-driven metabolic personalization offers strong competitive moats.
3. Recurring subscription revenue model with 1.3 million active subscribers, ~78% retention, strong LTV/CAC (15x), and diversified distribution channels.
4. Large and growing global nutraceutical market (~$250 billion) with favorable consumer health trends.
5. Robust free cash flow generation ($110M in 2025), strong balance sheet with modest net debt ensuring financial flexibility.
6. Well-defined growth plans including geographic expansion, product pipeline growth, B2B partnerships, and potential strategic acquisitions.
verdict.evidence_contra:
1. Regulatory risks given evolving compliance requirements for novel peptide supplements could impact speed to market or costs.
2. Competitive pressure from emerging biotech startups in personalized nutrition could increase market share battles.
3. Dependency on continuous R&D and innovation to maintain differentiation.
4. Customer retention may face headwinds from pricing pressure or competitive subscription offers.
5. Expansion into international markets carries execution and localization risks.
6. Reliance on proprietary AI and biotech manufacturing partnerships that need to scale effectively.
verdict.similar_deals:
– General Atlantic’s $1.2B growth equity investment in BioPeptix, a biotech peptide supplements platform, in 2024.
– KKR’s $850M buyout of NutriGen AI, a personalized nutrition SaaS and supplement provider, in 2023.
– TPG Growth’s $600M growth round in Cognivita Health, a cognitive supplements and wellness subscription company, in 2025.
– Sequoia-led $400M Series D in MetaboLabs, focused on adaptive metabolic health supplements leveraging AI, in 2025.
verdict.key_information_requested:
– Detailed due diligence on patent portfolio strength and remaining patent life for key peptides.
– Regulatory compliance audit and any ongoing or potential regulatory challenges globally.
– Validation of AI platform technology, underlying data sources, and scalability.
– Customer cohort analysis to confirm retention, churn drivers, and LTV stability.
– Manufacturing and supply chain capacity to scale production for international expansion.
– Detailed competitive landscape analysis including emerging startups and potential disruptors.
– Management team assessment and governance structure review.
